summary: This is a brief announcement post for The Talk Show's special WWDC 2020 episode featuring Apple executives Craig Federighi and Greg Joswiak as guests. The episode covers the major announcements from WWDC 2020, including the Mac's transition to Apple silicon, MacOS 11 Big Sur, and iOS/iPadOS 14. Gruber acknowledges the show's different format due to the pandemic, noting it was done remotely rather than as the traditional live event. The post is essentially promotional, listing sponsors and linking to the podcast.
key_points: The Talk Show featured Apple SVPs Craig Federighi and Greg Joswiak discussing WWDC 2020 announcements
Key topics included the Mac's transition to Apple silicon, MacOS 11 Big Sur, and iOS/iPadOS 14
The show was done remotely rather than as the traditional live audience format due to the pandemic
money_quotes: It's a very different show this year, but I think you'll enjoy it.
key_insight: WWDC 2020 marked a pivotal moment with Apple silicon's announcement, significant enough to warrant a remote edition of The Talk Show with two of Apple's most senior executives.
